machines.appliesTo: IntentObject

transitions.guard: Originating expression exists
Objective and known ambiguities recorded
Outcomes, non-goals, and constraints defined
Authorized decision exists
Change rationale and impact analysis initiated
Revised intent receives authorization
Replacement intent identified
transitions.note: VERBATIM §6.2. 'Any active' = any non-terminal state (RAW, UNDER_DISCOVERY, PROVISIONAL, FORMALIZED, APPROVED, REVISED).

transitions.guard: Authorized actor
transitions.note: VERBATIM §6.2. Withdraw permitted only from these three early states.

illegal.reason: §6.3: a superseded intent cannot authorize new PWUs. Prohibition on a terminal state, not an intra-machine transition.

machines.name: PWU.workLifecycleState
machines.appliesTo: Professional Work Unit (PWU) — aggregate root of the Work Aggregate (§3.1, §7.1). MERGED from both PWU-AXES and OTHER-OBJECTS extractions (identical substance).

transitions.guard: Intent exists
Shape readiness policy satisfied (§9 Shape Readiness Profile)
Active execution plan approved
Runtime bindings authorized
CROSS-AXIS guard: executionState=SUCCEEDED. Success does NOT auto-satisfy assurance (P1/INV-5).
transitions.note: §8.1 — doc-explicit coupling of workLifecycle to the executionState axis.

transitions.guard: Required evidence available or deficit explicitly recorded
Conditional disposition exists
CROSS-AXIS guard: assuranceState=SATISFIED. This is the ONLY legal path into workLifecycle SATISFIED (P1/INV-5).
Parent exists and recomposition is required
Recomposition contract satisfied
Authorized promotion decision
Authorized promotion decision

transitions.note: §8.2 row 'UNDER_ASSURANCE → REJECTED or RESHAPING' — one trigger, two legal targets (branch: REJECTED).
§8.2 row 'UNDER_ASSURANCE → REJECTED or RESHAPING' — one trigger, two legal targets (branch: RESHAPING).

transitions.guard: Authorized decision (Decision.decisionType=ABANDON)
transitions.note: §8.2 umbrella source — 'Any active' = any non-terminal state; kept verbatim, not enumerated per-state.

transitions.guard: Not already BASELINED
transitions.note: §8.2 umbrella source — 'Any non-baselined' = every state except BASELINED; kept verbatim.

illegal.reason: §8.3 — must be rejected; skips SHAPING→READY→PLANNED gating.
§8.3 — must be rejected.
§8.3 — must be rejected.

§8.3 'EXECUTING → SATISFIED without assurance' — encodes P1/INV-5 (§35.1 'No execution implies assurance'). Execution success alone can NEVER produce SATISFIED; only UNDER_ASSURANCE→SATISFIED with assuranceState=SATISFIED is legal.
§8.3 — must be rejected. NOTE: FAILED is an executionState value (§7.3), NOT a workLifecycleState; the doc lists this cross-axis rule here — a failed execution can never yield a SATISFIED PWU.
§8.3 — must be rejected.
§8.3 — must be rejected.
§8.3 — must be rejected.
§8.3 — must be rejected 'without creating a new revision or successor PWU'. An authoritative baseline is immutable (§24.2); re-execution requires a new revision/successor.

machines.name: PWU.executionState
machines.appliesTo: Professional Work Unit (PWU) — independent execution axis (§7.1 field executionState). Distinct from ExecutionStep.stepState (§21) and ExecutionPlan.status (§20.1).
machines.sourceSection: core-model doc §7.3 enum (VERBATIM). NO from→to matrix in doc; transitions RECONSTRUCTED from §26.4 events + enum semantics.

transitions.guard: step outputs recorded or explicit no-output
transitions.note: RECONSTRUCTED. SUCCEEDED is the guard for workLifecycle EXECUTING→EVIDENCE_PENDING (§8.1). MUST NOT auto-imply assuranceState=SATISFIED (P1/INV-5).
RECONSTRUCTED. Both RUNNING and RETRYING are valid from-states.
RECONSTRUCTED. All four named from-states are in the states list.
RECONSTRUCTED; superseded plan cannot create new attempts (§20.2). 'Any active' = any non-terminal state.

machines.name: PWU.assuranceState
machines.appliesTo: Professional Work Unit (PWU) — independent assurance axis (§7.1 field assuranceState). Rolled-up axis on the PWU. Distinct from AssuranceAssessment.disposition (§18) and AssuranceAssessment.state (§30 assurance doc).
machines.sourceSection: core-model doc §7.4 enum (VERBATIM). NO from→to matrix; transitions RECONSTRUCTED from §18 dispositions + §26.5 events + Scenario 3 (§39).

transitions.guard: independence requirements checked before evaluation (§18.1)
criteria met identified; evidence considered identified (§18.1) — NOT forced by executionState=SUCCEEDED (P1/INV-5)

transitions.note: RECONSTRUCTED; Scenario 3 (§39): execution SUCCEEDED + assurance REJECTED ⇒ PWU NOT SATISFIED.

transitions.guard: waiver authority separately defined (§18.1)
transitions.note: RECONSTRUCTED. All three named from-states are in the states list.
RECONSTRUCTED. Both named from-states are in the states list.

illegal.reason: CRITICAL — property P1 / INV-5 / §35.1 'No execution implies assurance'. ExecutionState=SUCCEEDED must NEVER automatically imply/force assuranceState=SATISFIED. The two axes are independent (§7.1, §38, §42 litmus #3). Corroborated by §39 Scenario 3 (execution SUCCEEDED while assurance REJECTED; PWU NOT SATISFIED) and §8.3 'EXECUTING→SATISFIED without assurance'. Assurance SATISFIED requires evaluated claims + admissible evidence + criteria met, independent of execution outcome. (Cross-axis coupling stated as a forbidden edge; the from-token names the executionState axis, not a state within this machine.)

transitions.note: RECONSTRUCTED. Both named from-states are in the states list.

machines.sourceSection: core-model doc §17.1 enum + assurance doc §3.1 enum (both VERBATIM, same states). NO lifecycle events in either doc's §26; transitions RECONSTRUCTED from enum semantics.

transitions.trigger: new policy version supersedes (assessments pin policySemanticVersion, §18)
transitions.note: RECONSTRUCTED. Both named from-states are in the states list.

machines.name: AssuranceAssessment.disposition
machines.appliesTo: AssuranceAssessment (aggregate root of the Assurance aggregate, §3.2) — VERDICT projection. Related to but distinct from AssuranceAssessment.state (§30 assurance doc) — see conflicts.
machines.sourceSection: core-model doc §18 enum (VERBATIM). Transitions RECONSTRUCTED from §26.5 events + §34.2 commands + §18.1 invariants. NO explicit matrix.

transitions.guard: independence requirements must be checked BEFORE evaluation begins (§18.1)
must identify policy version used, evidence considered, and criteria met (§18.1) — must NOT be forced by executionState=SUCCEEDED (P1/INV-5)
an inconclusive disposition cannot be treated as satisfied (§18.1)
a policy cannot waive its own blocking finding unless waiver authority is separately defined (§18.1)
transitions.note: RECONSTRUCTED. All three named from-states are in the states list.

illegal.reason: §18.1: an inconclusive disposition cannot be treated as satisfied.

PROPERTY P1 / INV-5 (§35.1): execution success must never automatically imply a satisfied assessment. Cross-axis forbidden edge; the from-token names the executionState axis, not a state in this machine.

machines.name: AssuranceAssessment.state
machines.appliesTo: A single assurance assessment instance evaluating one policy version against a subject's semantic version (per-assessment lifecycle FSM; assurance-service doc §30). This is the AUTHORITATIVE full FSM; AssuranceAssessment.disposition (§18) is its verdict projection.
machines.sourceSection: assurance-service doc §30 (states/transitions, the ONLY object with a drawn FSM in that doc) + §29 pipeline + §31 events + §32 commands + §34 error handling + §8.4 independence + §10 dispositions + §12 waivers + §39 invariants

transitions.trigger: AssuranceAssessmentRequested; claims instantiated, evidence requirements evaluated, missing evidence requested (AssuranceEvidenceRequired)
transitions.guard: one or more required EvidenceRequirements not yet satisfied
all required evidence present and admissible per §6.2 (identity stable, provenance present, in-scope, not invalidated, sufficiently current, relevant)
transitions.note: §30. READY = evidence satisfied + validator selected + independence checked, pre-execution.

transitions.guard: validator selected AND required independence verified (AssuranceIndependenceVerified) AND context assembled
transitions.note: §30 / §29 pipeline: validator selected → independence checked → context assembled → validator executes.

transitions.guard: all mandatory criteria MET; no open BLOCKING/CRITICAL finding; required independence verified (§8.4, INV-8); every satisfied claim references admissible evidence (INV-3); required evidence admissible (§10.3, §15.9)
transitions.note: §30. Authoritative disposition set by the Assurance Service, NOT by the validator recommendation (§4.2).

transitions.guard: claims supportable only if explicit conditions remain true or required follow-up occurs; typically an open MATERIAL finding (§10.1, §10.3)
open CRITICAL finding (→REJECTED or ESCALATED) OR open BLOCKING finding OR a material claim unsupported/contradicted/violates a blocking criterion (§10.1, §10.3, INV-11)
available admissible evidence insufficient to support or reject the claim; evidence deficit (§10.1, §10.3); also on evidence access failure (§34.3)
validator or policy cannot resolve the issue within its authority/competence (§10.1); may also be the disposition for an open CRITICAL finding (§10.3)

WaiverRule.waiverAllowed; criterion ∈ eligibleCriteriaIds; finding severity ∉ prohibitedFindingSeverities; required authority present; rationale/compensating controls recorded (§12). NOT waivable: unauthorized intent alteration (§15.11), critical baseline-integrity failures by ordinary authority (§26.7)
transitions.note: §30 lists WAIVED as a direct ASSESSING outcome. WaiverDenied (denyAssuranceWaiver) leaves the prior disposition unchanged (no drawn edge). A waiver never erases a finding (INV-13).

transitions.guard: validator crashed / timed out / errored during execution
transitions.note: §30. INV-9: a validator failure is NOT an assurance rejection. Next action = RETRY / alternate validator / ESCALATE (spawns a new assessment).

transitions.guard: producer and evaluator share invocation/agent/model/provider/hidden-context/prompt-lineage/authority in violation of policy's IndependenceRequirement (§8.2)
transitions.note: §30. Creates an Independence Violation Observation; another evaluator must be invoked or a waiver obtained (§8.4). Cannot receive SATISFIED (INV-8).

transitions.trigger: evidence access failure — required evidence cannot be retrieved (§34.3)
transitions.guard: required evidence becomes unavailable during assessment
transitions.note: NOT drawn in the §30 diagram; grounded in §34.3 (disposition becomes EVIDENCE_PENDING or INCONCLUSIVE; do not infer evidence content).

transitions.guard: subject semantic version changed (INV-16) OR supporting evidence invalidated (INV-15)
transitions.note: §30. Triggers reassessment of dependent claims; a fresh assessment must run — not a self-revert to SATISFIED.

transitions.guard: subject semantic version changed (INV-16) OR supporting evidence invalidated (INV-15) OR a required condition ceased to hold

WaiverRule.maximumDuration / recorded expiration reached, OR revalidationTrigger fired, OR a new subject semantic version (waiver does not apply to future semantic versions unless explicitly renewed — §12.2, INV-14)
assessment is in a non-terminal (active) state
transitions.note: §30 'ANY ACTIVE → CANCELLED' wildcard. ACTIVE = {REQUESTED, EVIDENCE_PENDING, READY, ASSESSING} — each may transition directly to CANCELLED.

illegal.reason: Forbidden when a mandatory criterion is NOT met even if the validator recommends SATISFIED — the Assurance Service must reject the recommendation (§4.2, Test 3, §10.3). 'Unable to determine' cannot be treated as 'met' (INV-6). Confidence cannot substitute for evidence (INV-7).
A validator failure is NOT an assurance rejection (§34.1; INV-9). It yields VALIDATOR_FAILED + retry/alternate-validator/escalation, never an authoritative REJECTED.
If required independence is not satisfied, the assessment cannot receive SATISFIED (§8.4; INV-8). Another independent evaluator must be invoked or a waiver obtained first.

CROSS-AXIS (P1/INV-5): executionState=SUCCEEDED must NEVER force assuranceState=SATISFIED. Execution success leaves assurance UNASSESSED until required policies complete (Test 9); a baseline cannot be promoted solely because all execution steps completed (INV-20); execution and assurance must use different visual indicators (§38). A successful execution trace proves execution occurred, not that the outcome satisfies intent (§6.3).

Malformed/incomplete (invalid) validator output cannot mutate authoritative state (INV-10; §34.2). Invalid output must be rejected at the boundary; no authoritative disposition may be created from unparsed prose.

transitions.trigger: proposeExecutionPlan / ExecutionPlanProposed then submitted for review

transitions.guard: plan approval required before irreversible execution for high-risk work; approval grants NO runtime privileges (§20.2)

must record outputs or an explicit no-output result; step success does NOT imply PWU success (§21.1)
a skipped MANDATORY step requires an authorized plan revision or waiver (§21.1)
transitions.note: RECONSTRUCTED. Both named from-states are in the states list.
RECONSTRUCTED. All four named from-states are in the states list.

transitions.guard: approval requires authority; an agent may recommend but cannot exercise authority unless delegated (§23.2)
transitions.note: RECONSTRUCTED; DecisionApproved and DecisionEffective both map onto the EFFECTIVE status.

machines.name: Baseline.status
machines.appliesTo: BaselineObject (aggregate root of the Baseline aggregate, §3.5).
machines.sourceSection: core-model doc §24.1 enum (VERBATIM). Transitions RECONSTRUCTED from §26.6 events + §34.4 commands + §24.2 invariants. NO explicit matrix.

transitions.guard: open BLOCKING observations prevent promotion unless waived (§24.2)
requires an explicit EFFECTIVE promotion decision (§23.2, §24.2); promotion evidence must be retained; promotion is a governance event, not an execution step (§24.2)
an authoritative baseline is immutable — changes create a SUCCESSOR baseline; supersession preserves traceability (§24.2)

transitions.note: RECONSTRUCTED. Both named from-states are in the states list.

illegal.reason: §24.2 + §35.1 'No unauthorized baseline': a baseline cannot become AUTHORITATIVE without an effective promotion decision (must be APPROVED + carry a promotion decision); open blocking observations block promotion unless waived.
§24.2: an authoritative baseline is IMMUTABLE — it cannot be re-promoted/mutated in place; changes create a successor baseline.

machines.name: AggregateAssuranceDisposition
machines.appliesTo: Computed roll-up/reduction across ALL policies applicable to one object (assurance-service doc §28.2). NOT an event-driven lifecycle — each row is condition → aggregate state. Introduces two states (EVIDENCE_REQUIRED, UNASSESSED) not present in the per-assessment §30 machine.
machines.sourceSection: assurance-service doc §28.2 (aggregate disposition) + §28.1 (composition rules). 'This must not be reduced to a numerical average.'

transitions.guard: any critical rejection OR any blocking rejection among applicable policies
transitions.note: §28.2. Strictest unresolved disposition wins (§28.1); a satisfied advisory policy does not override a rejected blocking policy. Pseudo-source: this is a reduction, not an FSM edge.

transitions.guard: any required assessment missing (evidence-deficit variant)
transitions.note: §28.2 lists 'EVIDENCE_REQUIRED or UNASSESSED' for a missing required assessment — see open items.

transitions.guard: any required assessment missing / not yet run
any inconclusive required assessment (and no rejection / missing assessment of higher precedence)
any conditional required assessment (and none of the higher-precedence conditions hold)
all required assessments satisfied

illegal.reason: Aggregate must preserve the strictest unresolved disposition (§28.1); it must NOT be reduced to a numerical average of policy results (§28.2). Conflicting assessments must remain visible, not silently averaged (INV-17, §34.4). I.e. SATISFIED is forbidden whenever any higher-precedence unresolved condition holds.

machines.name: ValidatorRegistryEntry.status
machines.appliesTo: Operational status of a registered validator in the Validator Registry (assurance-service doc §35).
machines.sourceSection: assurance-service doc §35 enum (VERBATIM). NO transition table specified in the doc.

conflicts.topic: CRITICAL RULE PRESERVED — executionState=SUCCEEDED must NEVER force assuranceState=SATISFIED (property P1 / INV-5). Not a disagreement; all three extractions agree. Recorded here to confirm the reconciliation kept it on every surface.
conflicts.resolution: Encoded consistently in FOUR machines: (1) PWU.workLifecycleState.illegal — 'EXECUTING→SATISFIED' and 'FAILED→SATISFIED' (§8.3); (2) PWU.assuranceState.illegal — 'executionState=SUCCEEDED → SATISFIED'; (3) AssuranceAssessment.disposition.illegal — 'executionState=SUCCEEDED → SATISFIED'; (4) AssuranceAssessment.state.illegal — 'ASSESSING→SATISFIED (cross-axis)'. The four PWU axes (workLifecycleState, executionState, assuranceState, shapeIntegrityState) remain INDEPENDENT §7.1 fields. The ONLY doc-explicit cross-axis couplings are the two §8.1 guards: EXECUTING→EVIDENCE_PENDING guarded by executionState=SUCCEEDED, and UNDER_ASSURANCE→SATISFIED guarded by assuranceState=SATISFIED. Neither collapses the axes; both are one-directional preconditions.

conflicts.topic: PWU.executionState / assuranceState / shapeIntegrityState: transitions VERBATIM-empty vs RECONSTRUCTED. The PWU-AXES extraction left transitions empty (spec defines these three axes ONLY as §7.3/§7.4/§7.5 state enums, no from→to matrix); the OTHER-OBJECTS extraction reconstructed edges from §26 events + invariants.
conflicts.resolution: KEPT the reconstructed transitions (they are the best available and are event/invariant-grounded) but every such row is marked note:'RECONSTRUCTED'. The STATE LISTS are verbatim and authoritative; the EDGES are engineering inference and must be validated against the §26 event catalog before the kernel enforces them as a hard contract. No workLifecycle-style illegal table exists for these three axes except the P1/INV-5 cross-axis rule.

conflicts.topic: AssuranceAssessment modeled twice by two different source documents: core-model §18 'disposition' (8 states) vs assurance-service §30 'state' (15 states incl. WAIVER_EXPIRED).
conflicts.resolution: KEPT as two separate machine entries because they have different axis names (.disposition vs .state), different cardinality, and different section sources. The §30 15-state FSM is the AUTHORITATIVE implementation lifecycle; its outcome states {SATISFIED, CONDITIONALLY_SATISFIED, REJECTED, INCONCLUSIVE, ESCALATED, WAIVED} are a superset of the §18 verdict. Recommendation: kernel implements the §30 FSM and derives the §18 .disposition as its verdict projection (§18 adds no states the §30 machine lacks, except it uses PENDING where §30 uses the richer REQUESTED/EVIDENCE_PENDING/READY pre-assessment states).

conflicts.topic: AssurancePolicy.status (core-model §17.1) vs AssurancePolicyDefinition.status (assurance-service §3.1): same 4-state enum DRAFT/ACTIVE/SUSPENDED/SUPERSEDED under two names in two docs.
conflicts.resolution: MERGED into a single machine 'AssurancePolicy.status'. Transitions are reconstructed (neither doc defines policy-lifecycle events). sourceSection cites both § references.

conflicts.topic: Section-number NAMESPACE COLLISION between the two source documents. The core-model doc and the assurance-service doc each restart section numbering, so identical labels denote different content (e.g., core §8 = PWU workLifecycle; assurance §8.4 = independence. core §10 = Obligation; assurance §10 = dispositions. core §12 = Assumption; assurance §12 = waivers. core §18 = AssuranceAssessment.disposition; §30 is assurance-only).
conflicts.resolution: Every machine's sourceSection is now prefixed with its origin doc ('core-model doc' vs 'assurance-service doc'). Section refs MUST be namespaced by source; do not cross-wire them. Also: the task's example label 'DOC-002 §8.3' does NOT correspond to any label in either file — both are self-contained specs and §8.3 (core doc) is the ONLY enumerated illegal-transition table.

conflicts.topic: PWU.shapeIntegrityState: is RESTORED terminal? PWU-AXES marked terminalStates=[RESTORED]; OTHER-OBJECTS reconstructed a RESTORED→PRESERVED re-validation edge (making it non-terminal) and left terminalStates=[].
conflicts.resolution: Set terminalStates=[] and kept the RESTORED→PRESERVED edge, because the repair-loop reading (RESTORED re-validates back to PRESERVED) is more consistent with the reconstructed RESHAPING_REQUIRED→RESHAPING_IN_PROGRESS→RESTORED chain. Neither is doc-authoritative (no §7.5 matrix); flagged as inference. If the kernel prefers RESTORED as absorbing, drop that one edge.

conflicts.topic: PWU.workLifecycleState compound source rows. §8.1 'SATISFIED/RECOMPOSED → BASELINED', §8.2 'PLANNED/EXECUTING → BLOCKED', and §8.2 'UNDER_ASSURANCE → REJECTED or RESHAPING' were represented as split single-state edges in one extraction and compound rows in the other.
conflicts.resolution: Used the SPLIT form so no transition names a compound pseudo-state: SATISFIED→BASELINED + RECOMPOSED→BASELINED; PLANNED→BLOCKED + EXECUTING→BLOCKED; UNDER_ASSURANCE→REJECTED + UNDER_ASSURANCE→RESHAPING. The umbrella from-states 'Any active' (→ABANDONED) and 'Any non-baselined' (→SUPERSEDED) are kept verbatim (NOT enumerated per-state) because the doc states them as umbrellas; their set definitions are in each row's note.

openItems.item: PWU.assuranceState initial state is genuinely ambiguous (NOT_REQUIRED vs UNASSESSED).
openItems.why: The doc labels neither §7.4 value as initial. NOT_REQUIRED (no policy applies) and UNASSESSED (assurance pending) are both legitimate entry points. Defaulted to NOT_REQUIRED, but the kernel must pick per-PWU based on whether any AssurancePolicy applies to the subject; picking wrong silently changes whether the P1/INV-5 gate is even armed.

openItems.item: Evidence emits an EvidenceExpired event (§26.5) but §16.1 status enum has NO EXPIRED value.
openItems.why: Expiration (§16.2 triggers reassessment of dependent claims) currently has no target status. Folded into ADMISSIBLE→INVALIDATED, but it could equally map to SUPERSEDED. Kernel must decide and document the mapping; downstream claim CONTESTED logic depends on it.

openItems.item: Decision emits DecisionApproved and DecisionRejected events (§26.6) but §23.1 enum has only PROPOSED/EFFECTIVE/REVOKED/SUPERSEDED.
openItems.why: Approval maps to EFFECTIVE, but a rejected decision has no status value — it presumably stays PROPOSED or moves to SUPERSEDED (currently mapped PROPOSED→SUPERSEDED). Kernel must define the rejected-decision resting state; governance authority checks read this field.

openItems.item: Three PWU axes (executionState, assuranceState, shapeIntegrityState) have NO verbatim transition matrix — all their edges are reconstructed.
openItems.why: The spec guarantees only the state enums. Enforcing the reconstructed edges as hard invariants risks blocking valid event sequences the doc never forbade. Validate each reconstructed edge against the §26 event catalog before the kernel treats it as a contract; treat them as advisory until then.

openItems.item: AggregateAssuranceDisposition (§28.2) lists both EVIDENCE_REQUIRED and UNASSESSED for the same 'missing required assessment' condition.
openItems.why: Precedence/selection between the two is unspecified. The reduction must preserve the strictest unresolved disposition and must NOT be a numerical average (§28.1, INV-17). Kernel needs a deterministic tie-break rule (likely EVIDENCE_REQUIRED when an EvidenceRequirement exists but is unmet; UNASSESSED when no assessment was ever started).

openItems.item: PWU.workLifecycleState recovery/outgoing edges from non-terminal EXCEPTION states (BLOCKED, CHALLENGED, RESHAPING, ESCALATED, REJECTED, INVALIDATED) are NOT in the §8 matrix.
openItems.why: The §8 tables enumerate how you ENTER these states but not how you leave them. Recovery is governed by Controller control actions (§37: RESHAPE_PWU, REPLAN_EXECUTION, REVISE_DECOMPOSITION, ESCALATE, WAIVE, REJECT, ABANDON) and invalidation outcomes (§29.3) — a separate surface the kernel must source, or those states become dead-ends.

openItems.item: AssuranceAssessment.state VALIDATOR_FAILED and INDEPENDENCE_VIOLATION have no in-machine recovery arrow (§30).
openItems.why: Retry / alternate-validator / escalation / waiver (§34.1, §8.4) spawn a NEW assessment instance rather than transitioning the failed one. Kernel must model recovery as new-instance creation, not a self-loop; both states are terminal for THAT assessment instance.
